REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT PAUL IRACKI, PAULA IRACKI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD (NO SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION) AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS (THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT AVAILABLE UNDER THE FACTS OF THIS CASE; ADDITIONALLY DAMAGES FOR MENTAL DISTRESS MAY BE RECOVERED PER NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION, WHICH IS SEPARATELY ALLEGED. SEE CRISCI V. SECURITY INS. CO. (1967) 66 CAL.2D 425 AND POTTER V. FIRESTONE (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 965). DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED - CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED; AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION, RETALIATORY EVICTION AND CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION - ALLEGATIONS ARE SUFFICIENT AND COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT SFRO 37.9(A)(9) WAS IMPROPERLY ASSERTED IN OCTOBER 2014, WHILE THE PREMISES WERE OFFERED FOR RENT IN JULY 2015. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Continued To Jun-08-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Spencer Martinez, (415)543-4800 for Defendant/Moving party Paul Iracki.

Not argured. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PAUL IRACKI, PAULA IRACKI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurer is sustained without leave to amend to causes of action for fraud (no substantive opposition) and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress (this cause of action is not available under the facts of this case; additionally damages for mental distress may be recovered per Negligence cause of action, which is separately alleged. See Crisci v. Security Ins. Co. (1967) 66 Cal.2d 425 and Potter v. Firestone (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965). Demurrer is otherwise overruled - cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is sufficiently alleged; as to the causes of action for wrongful eviction, retaliatory eviction and constructive eviction - allegations are sufficient and complaint alleges that SFRO 37.9(a)(9) was improperly asserted in October 2014, while the premises were offered for rent in July 2015. Order signed. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
